title: Harden tree file operations and path containment
description:
Created from the project state audit.

Fix rename/path safety and stale Pinake UI state after tree file operations.

Context:
- `rename` currently validates only non-empty input, so path separators, traversal segments, and invalid document names are not blocked consistently.
- `toWorkspaceRelative` falls back to a basename when a URI is outside the expected root, which can hide containment bugs instead of failing closed.
- Rename/delete can leave favorites, expanded/collapsed paths, and last-opened state stale.

Implementation notes:
- Reuse the stricter new-file/new-folder validation rules for rename based on node kind.
- Reject operations whose source or target is outside `.pinake/docs`.
- Make relative-path conversion fail closed for out-of-root URIs.
- Update favorites, expanded/collapsed paths, and last-opened state after rename/delete.
- Add extension-host tests for traversal rejection, state cleanup, favorite preservation, manifest updates, and index updates.

Code anchors: `src/commands/PinakesCommands.ts:405`, `src/services/uriUtils.ts:18`.

checklist.text: Add fail-closed path containment helpers for `.pinake/docs` operations
Apply file/folder rename validation by node kind
Clean up favorites, expanded/collapsed paths, and last-opened state after rename/delete
Add command tests for traversal rejection, state cleanup, manifest updates, and index updates
